{"id":"turtlebot-maze-c1m","title":"Incorporate AWS RoboMaker bookstore world as a third simulation environment","description":"Integrate the AWS RoboMaker bookstore world (Gazebo Harmonic migration, PR#21 by binner2) as a third launchable environment alongside maze and house worlds.

Source: https://github.com/aws-robotics/aws-robomaker-bookstore-world/pull/21
- Full migration from Gazebo Classic to Gazebo Harmonic (SDF 1.9, DART physics)
- 38 retail models: bookshelves (A/B/C), books (A-F), info desk, columns, chairs, computers, etc.
- Updated mesh URIs from file://models/ to model:// format

Implementation (mirrors house world approach):
- Clone PR#21 branch and copy models to tb_worlds/models/aws_robomaker_retail_*/
- Adapt worlds/bookstore.world → tb_worlds/worlds/bookstore_world.sdf.xacro
  (add xacro headless arg + gz-sim Harmonic plugin declarations)
- Copy or generate Nav2 occupancy map → tb_worlds/maps/bookstore_world_map.{pgm,yaml}
- Add demo-world-bookstore service to docker-compose.yaml
  (same overlay image as house/maze — no rebuild needed)
- Set GZ_SIM_RESOURCE_PATH to include models dir

The bookstore world features a retail shop interior with bookshelves as navigation
obstacles — good for testing goal-to-goal navigation in a structured commercial space.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"pantelis.monogioudis@aegean.ai","created_at":"2026-03-02T14:17:14Z","created_by":"Pantelis Monogioudis","updated_at":"2026-03-02T14:17:14Z"}

{"id":"turtlebot-maze-7s7","title":"Generate Nav2 map for house environment","description":"Create a 2D occupancy grid map for the house world to be used by Nav2 AMCL localisation:
- Run slam_toolbox or cartographer in mapping mode within the house Gazebo world
- Manually drive TurtleBot3 through all rooms to build the map
- Save map as tb_worlds/maps/house_map.yaml + house_map.pgm
- Tune map resolution and free/occupied thresholds
- Verify map coverage: kitchen, living room, doorway corridor all visible
This map is consumed by the Nav2 amcl node at runtime.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"pantelis.monogioudis@aegean.ai","created_at":"2026-03-02T12:30:10Z","created_by":"Pantelis Monogioudis","updated_at":"2026-03-02T12:30:10Z"}

Key gotcha: DeskPortrait/Portrait models in the house world use relative path ../../../../photos/ from their meshes dirs — fixed by adding ./tb_worlds/photos:/overlay_ws/install/tb_worlds/share/photos:ro mount. Merged in PR#17 (house) and PR#18 (bookstore+warehouse)."}

{"id":"turtlebot-maze-a9a","title":"Add house-world docker-compose service","description":"Add a house-world service to docker-compose.yaml (alongside the renamed maze-world service):
- Service name: demo-world-house (or house-world)
- Launches Gazebo with house_world.sdf
- Launches Nav2 with nav2_params_house.yaml and house_map
- Launches behaviour demo nodes (same as maze-world)
- User selects world by running: docker compose up demo-world-house
- Shares common base service config with maze-world via YAML anchors/extends where possible
- Depends on: turtlebot-maze-ud7 (rename), turtlebot-maze-gr3 (SDF), turtlebot-maze-7s7 (map), turtlebot-maze-v6k (Nav2 params)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"pantelis.monogioudis@aegean.ai","created_at":"2026-03-02T12:30:22Z","created_by":"Pantelis Monogioudis","updated_at":"2026-03-02T12:30:22Z"}

{"notes":"Delivered three new simulation environments using AWS RoboMaker open-source worlds ported to Gazebo Harmonic / ROS 2 Jazzy: (1) demo-world-house: residential interior, 68 models, 5cm/px map (PR#17); (2) demo-world-bookstore: retail store, 34 models, 5cm/px map (PR#18); (3) demo-world-warehouse: industrial no-roof, 14 models, 2cm/px map (PR#18). Key lessons: volume mount pattern injects world/model/map files at runtime without image rebuild; DeskPortrait models use relative ../../../../photos/ paths requiring a share/photos/ install target; each world's map is passed as a launch arg to tb_demo_world.launch.py. {"id":"turtlebot-maze-98z","title":"Create house environment with kitchen and living room for TurtleBot navigation","description":"Design and implement a Gazebo house environment featuring a kitchen and living room where the TurtleBot3 can navigate autonomously, similar to the existing maze world. The environment should:
- Include realistic room layouts (kitchen, living room, connecting hallway)
- Have navigable open floor plans suitable for TurtleBot3 footprint
- Include furniture as obstacles (tables, chairs, counters, sofa, coffee table)
- Provide a Nav2-compatible map and costmap configuration
- Support the existing behavior tree and autonomy stack without modification
- Include colored block targets placeable in different rooms for search tasks","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"pantelis.monogioudis@aegean.ai","created_at":"2026-03-02T12:24:30Z","created_by":"Pantelis Monogioudis","updated_at":"2026-03-02T12:24:30Z"}

new_value: Epic complete — three environments delivered and merged to main.

{"notes":"Decision: Use open_clip (ViT-B-32, laion2b_s34b_b79k pretrained) for 512-dim CLIP embeddings. YOLOv8 detects+crops, CLIP encodes each crop. Library: open_clip_torch (pip). Embedding included in Zenoh tb/detections payload as base64-encoded float32 array.","status":"in_progress"}
old_value:
{"id":"turtlebot-maze-9k0","title":"Extract feature embeddings from YOLOv8 backbone for detected objects","description":"The object_detector.py currently runs YOLOv8 inference and publishes bbox+class+confidence via Zenoh (tb/detections). For assignment-3 semantic re-localization, we need to also extract a feature vector (embedding) for each detected object crop.

YOLOv8 architecture layers to consider:
- **Backbone (CSPDarknet)**: Produces spatial feature maps at multiple scales. The final backbone output (before neck) gives a rich semantic representation.
- **Neck (SPPF + PAN-FPN)**: Fuses multi-scale features. Intermediate outputs here are detection-oriented.
- **Head**: Classification + regression heads — too task-specific for general embeddings.

Options:
1. **Crop + CLIP**: Crop each detection bbox from the original frame, run through a CLIP image encoder → 512-dim vector. This is what assignment-3 specifies and gives semantically rich embeddings comparable across object classes.
2. **Backbone feature extraction**: Hook into YOLOv8 backbone (e.g. model.model.model[9] — SPPF output) and RoI-pool per detection. Faster but less semantic than CLIP.
3. **Hybrid**: Use YOLOv8 for detection, CLIP for embedding — two models but best quality.

Decision needed: CLIP (assignment-3 preferred) vs backbone features vs hybrid. CLIP is the assignment spec default.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"pantelis.monogioudis@aegean.ai","created_at":"2026-03-23T21:39:38Z","created_by":"Pantelis Monogioudis","updated_at":"2026-03-23T21:39:38Z"}

new_value:
{"notes":"Implementation complete. Changes:
- detector/object_detector.py: Added CLIP embedding extraction (open_clip ViT-B-32/laion2b_s34b_b79k). Each detection crop is batch-encoded, L2-normalized, and serialized as base64 float32 in the Zenoh payload. New flags: --enable-embeddings (default true), --clip-model, --clip-pretrained.
- detector/requirements.txt: Added open_clip_torch>=2.24.0
- docker/Dockerfile.torch.gpu: Added build-essential + Rust toolchain (eclipse-zenoh needs Rust to build from source), added CLIP weight pre-download layer.
- Docker image builds and includes both YOLO and CLIP weights."}
old_value:
{"id":"turtlebot-maze-9k0","title":"Extract feature embeddings from YOLOv8 backbone for detected objects","description":"The object_detector.py currently runs YOLOv8 inference and publishes bbox+class+confidence via Zenoh (tb/detections). For assignment-3 semantic re-localization, we need to also extract a feature vector (embedding) for each detected object crop.

YOLOv8 architecture layers to consider:
- **Backbone (CSPDarknet)**: Produces spatial feature maps at multiple scales. The final backbone output (before neck) gives a rich semantic representation.
- **Neck (SPPF + PAN-FPN)**: Fuses multi-scale features. Intermediate outputs here are detection-oriented.
- **Head**: Classification + regression heads — too task-specific for general embeddings.

Options:
1. **Crop + CLIP**: Crop each detection bbox from the original frame, run through a CLIP image encoder → 512-dim vector. This is what assignment-3 specifies and gives semantically rich embeddings comparable across object classes.
2. **Backbone feature extraction**: Hook into YOLOv8 backbone (e.g. model.model.model[9] — SPPF output) and RoI-pool per detection. Faster but less semantic than CLIP.
3. **Hybrid**: Use YOLOv8 for detection, CLIP for embedding — two models but best quality.

Decision needed: CLIP (assignment-3 preferred) vs backbone features vs hybrid. CLIP is the assignment spec default.","notes":"Decision: Use open_clip (ViT-B-32, laion2b_s34b_b79k pretrained) for 512-dim CLIP embeddings. YOLOv8 detects+crops, CLIP encodes each crop. Library: open_clip_torch (pip). Embedding included in Zenoh tb/detections payload as base64-encoded float32 array.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"pantelis.monogioudis@aegean.ai","created_at":"2026-03-23T21:39:38Z","created_by":"Pantelis Monogioudis","updated_at":"2026-03-23T21:44:44Z"}

{"id":"turtlebot-maze-l9b","title":"Wire Zenoh router to pgvector for detection embedding ingest","description":"Create a Zenoh subscriber (ingest worker) that:
1. Subscribes to tb/detections on the Zenoh router
2. For each detection, receives the embedding vector (from turtlebot-maze-9k0)
3. Writes the embedding + metadata (class, confidence, bbox, timestamp, pose) to the pgvector PostgreSQL service (vectordb on port 5436)

This is the pipeline: detector → Zenoh (tb/detections) → ingest worker → pgvector (detection_embeddings table).

The ingest worker should be a new Docker service in docker-compose.yaml, similar to detection-logger but writing to Postgres instead of JSONL. It needs:
- zenoh Python client
- psycopg2 or asyncpg for Postgres
- pgvector Python bindings
- Idempotent writes (upsert on detection key)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"pantelis.monogioudis@aegean.ai","created_at":"2026-03-23T21:39:48Z","created_by":"Pantelis Monogioudis","updated_at":"2026-03-23T21:39:48Z"}

{"notes":"Implementation complete. Changes:
- detector/embedding_ingest.py: New Zenoh subscriber that decodes base64 CLIP embeddings from tb/detections and inserts into pgvector (detection_embeddings table).
- vector-init.sql: Extended schema with class_name, confidence, bbox, embedding_model, ingested_at columns.
- detector/requirements.txt: Added psycopg2-binary>=2.9.0.
- docker-compose.yaml: New embedding-ingest service (depends_on vector + zenoh-router, network_mode host, PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1).
- Verified: container starts, connects to pgvector on port 5436, subscribes to tb/detections."}

{"id":"turtlebot-maze-j81","title":"Investigate Gaussian Splatting for house world navigation and semantic mapping","description":"Explore how 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) can complement or enhance the current semantic mapping pipeline in the house world environment.

Areas to investigate:
- 3DGS scene reconstruction from robot camera frames (D435i RGB-D)
- Novel view synthesis for visual re-localization (render-and-compare vs current CLIP KNN approach)
- Dense semantic 3D maps via feature-field splatting (e.g. LERF, LangSplat) — embedding CLIP/DINO features into Gaussians
- Real-time navigation using splat-based occupancy or traversability maps
- Incremental/online splatting as the robot explores (vs offline reconstruction)
- Integration with existing AGE semantic graph — can splat-derived geometry refine Place centroids or Object positions?
- Sim-to-real: training splats in Gazebo house world, transferring to physical environment
- Comparison with current ORB-SLAM3 + pgvector + AGE pipeline

References to review:
- 3D Gaussian Splatting (Kerbl et al. 2023)
- SplaTAM: Splat Track & Map (Keetha et al. 2024)
- LangSplat / LERF for language-embedded radiance fields
- GS-SLAM variants for real-time SLAM with Gaussians
- Nerfstudio / gsplat frameworks","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"pantelis.monogioudis@aegean.ai","created_at":"2026-03-24T21:24:29Z","created_by":"Pantelis Monogioudis","updated_at":"2026-03-24T21:24:29Z"}
